channel.title: Culture File with Luke Clancy
channel.description: Culture File and The Comfort Zone — RTÉ lyric fm with Luke Clancy
channel.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Ireland's Early Medieval Hand-Bells with Helen Flanagan
item.description: The Comfort Zone: Helen Flanagan of "art hag" duo, Spooky Beore, suggests Colm Tóibín and Luke Clancy spend some time contemplating the hand bells of medieval Ireland, and their many unintended uses, including in the laying on of curses.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Thierry Tidrow | Culture File Digital Single
item.description: Canadian composer, Thierry Tidrow who features in this year's New Music Dublin festival on the strange history of Claude Vivier, the art of capturing online speech in music, and his attraction to making opera for children.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Norma Winstone, Living On The Wind | Culture File
item.description: Legendary jazz vocalist Norma Winstone on learning to understand the voice as an instrument, the genius of bandmate, Kenny Wheeler, and how Drake became her most famous fan. Also, Paddy Woodwth awards Scott Weidensaul's Living on the Wind some cherished space on the Naturalist's Bookshelf.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Princess Mononoke: with Ishmael Claxton
item.description: The Comfort Zone: New York-born, Dublin-based fine artist and photographer Ishmael Claxton invites Colm and Luke to head to an imaginary, demon-and-talking-animal-filled Japan in the 14th century, to encounter a masterwork from the Japanese Disney, Studio Ghibli, directed by its founder, Hayao Miyazaki.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Ms Hartman's Neighbourhood | Culture File Digital Single
item.description: Composer, improvisor and evangelist for the power of children's music, Ríona Sally Hartman leads a tour of her musical world.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File Digital Single: What's Up With Tracey?
item.description: If you enjoyed Orit Gat's essay on Tracey Emin's sort-of-retrospective at Tate Modern (which you can hear in the current edition of Culture File), here is some further conversation between Orit Gat and Luke Clancy, around Emin, Autofiction, Bad Museums, Rose Wiley and Nobel Prize Winners.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Tracey at The Tate, Make2026, Patrick O'Laoghaire
item.description: Orit Gat takes in the new Tate Modern show from former YBA, now officially OBA, Tracey Emin; Rachel Andrews is at Cork's Make2026, with Prof Helen A. Fielding to learn which AI to love; and Patrick O'Laoghaire's Island postcard comes from a road near Clifden.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File Digital Single: Pick of The Week
item.description: Marking your cards for some time well spent, The Comfort Zone's Colm Tóibín suggests spending (quite a few) minutes with The Met's latest production of Tristran und Isolde (screening in select Irish cinemas this weekend); artist Rónán Ó Raghallaigh offers Carlo Ginsberg's The Cheese and The Worms; and Luke Clancy counters the two series of Lucia Keskin's sitcom, Things You Should have Done.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Future Library: with Maeve Stone
item.description: The Comfort Zone: Artist Maeve Stone champions Katie Paterson's deep time artwork, Future Library, and sparks a conversation with Colm Tóibín about space, eternity and marshmallows.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: A Tetrapod Makes Land: Culture File Digital Single
item.description: Events on Valentia Island around 360 million years ago set in motion an exhibition from artist Bryony Dunne, currently at the Irish Architectural Archive in Dublin.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Debate: What Is A Sound?
item.description: Sound artist and composer Tarek Atoui, musician and recordist Natalia Beylis, and Oxn drummer, Eleanor Myler probe the act of making a sound, and the art of receiving one. Recorded at IMMA, Kilmainham.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Attention and Will: with Meghan O'Gieblyn
item.description: Essayist, Meghan O'Gieblyn invites Colm Toibin and Luke Clancy to an encounter with French philosopher and mystic, Simone Weil, in particular her posthumously published essay/poem Attention and Will.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Crows and Corsets
item.description: Rion Hanora O'Donovan on the place of 'bad' graffiti in her clothes designs; Dermot Rogers puts some sunlight on the legacy of jazz guitarist, Louis Stewart; Paddy Woodworth slips Esther Woolfson on to the Naturalist's Bookshelf; and Patrick O'Laoghaire starts a new series musical Island postcards.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Great White Way: with Harold Offeh
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone. Artist and performer, Harold Offeh encourages Colm Tóibín and Luke Clancy to think/talk about the late US artist, William Pope.L's performance crawls, as well as the powers of lounging and of silliness.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: An Afternoon at the Opera
item.description: Culture File XXXL Edition | After a troubled Northern Irish childhood, Vivien Hewitt found a new life in Italy, and in opera. The director and designer takes a seat in the afternoon quiet of Trieste's Theatre Verdi to talk about a life in the dreamworld of opera. (Photo credit: Vivien Hewitt)
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Neu! with Eleanor Myler
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone. XXXL Edition. Drummer, vocalist and member of the danktrad supergroup, OXN, Eleanor Myler leads Colm Tóibín into the nice 'n' steady sound of krautrockers, Neu! (From 111025)
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Neu! with Eleanor Myler XXXL Edition
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone. Drummer, vocalist and member of the danktrad supergroup, OXN, Eleanor Myler leads Colm Tóibín into the nice 'n' steady sound of krautrockers, Neu! (From 111025)
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Debate: Quiet/Loud XXXL Edition
item.description: The Culture File panel explores the place of quiet and loud in the contemporary attention economy, with poet, Paula Meehan and musicians and composers, Siobhán Cleary, John Godfrey, and Christine Tobin. Recorded live at New Music Dublin 2025. (From 260425)
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Burn a Million Quid XXXL Edition
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone Artist and curator, Kerry Guinan join Colm Tóibín and Luke Clancy to rake over the embers of art-provocateurs K-Foundation's 1994 performance of burning £1 million on a Scottish island. (First broadcast July 25)
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Burn a Million Quid
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone XXXL Edition: Artist and curator, Kerry Guinan join Colm Tóibín and Luke Clancy to rake over the embers of art-provocateurs K-Foundation's 1994 performance of burning £1 million on a Scottish island. (First broadcast July 25)
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Debate: Wìngéd Muses
item.description: How birds and their metaphors move through the work and the lives of novelist, John Banville, art critic, Orit Gat, novelist, Sara Baume, journalist and author Paddy Woodworth. Under consideration are: The Pigeon; The Swift; The Wheatear; and The Crane. But which scribe fancies which wìngéd muse? (First broadcast Dec 24)
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone
item.description: In this XXXL edition of The Comfort Zone, The Rats: 'horrademic' Prof Darryl Jones brings a favourite 1970s gorecore novel into the Zone, with a steer that James Herbert's The Rats, racism, misogyny and homophobia notwithstanding, has something to offer the world of 2025.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Debate: The Hare's Corner
item.description: In this XXXL edition of the debate, an ancient Irish farming tradition that sees the wisdom of leaving some farm land free from the imperatives of production -- ‘The Hare’s Corner’ -- is celebrated in poetry, story, discussion and music. Paddy Woodworth leads a panel featuring Jane Clarke, Catherine Cleary, Jane Carkill and Colm Mac Con Iomaire.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Close Up Magic: with Michael Dervan XXXL Edition
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone. Music critic Michael Dervan wants everyone in the zone to enjoy the artistry of The Great Slydini and other masters of sleight of hand and misdirection.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Rats: 'horrademic'
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone The Rats: 'horrademic' Prof Darryl Jones brings a favourite 1970s gorecore novel into the Zone, with a steer that James Herbert's The Rats, racism, misogyny and homophobia notwithstanding, something to offer the world of 2025
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Rats: 'horrademic' XXXL Edition
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone The Rats: 'horrademic' Prof Darryl Jones brings a favourite 1970s gorecore novel into the Zone, with a steer that James Herbert's The Rats, racism, misogyny and homophobia notwithstanding, something to offer the world of 2025
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Close Up Magic: with Michael Dervan
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone. Music critic Michael Dervan wants everyone in the zone to enjoy the artistry of The Great Slydini and other masters of sleight of hand and misdirection.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Debate Extra: Freedom
item.description: Outlandish Theatre Company's performance event Freedom uses the myth of Antigone to explore the act of speaking about Gaza, about the limits of art and speech. Listen to the piece in its entirety here. Recorded live at Samuel Beckett Theatre, TCD, Dublin in November 2025.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Debate: Freedom?
item.description: Outlandish Theatre's latest, Freedom works with the myth of Antigone to focus on the act of speaking about Gaza, about the world in which it exists, and about the limits of art and speech. After the show's Dublin debut, the creative team gathered to explore further for the Culture File Debate. Check the Culture File feed for a live recording of the Freedom performance.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Reconstruction: with Joshua Barley
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone. Translator, Joshua Barley encourages Colm Tóibín to watch Reconstruction (Anaparastasi) Theo Angelopoulos' 1970 avant police procedural set in the rock-studded, murder-harbouring landscape of the Northern Greek province of Epirus.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Kerry James Marshall, Unmade Art, Ragas Live
item.description: 24 Hour Raga People at a festival in Redhook, NY; by the banks of the River Nore, Tadhg O'Sullivan journeys into art that might not get made ; and in Piccadilly, the largest ever European Survey for the veteran American, Kerry James Marshall, a painter of Black American life like no other.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Art of Listening: Under Water: with Finola Merivale - XXXL Edition
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone. Composer, Fiona Merivale encourages Colm Tóibín to tune into The Art of Listening: Under Water, a recent work by the legendary Norwegian recordist and composer, Jana Winderen.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Art of Listening: Under Water: with Finola Merivale
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone XXXL Edition. Composer, Fiona Merivale encourages Colm Tóibín to tune into The Art of Listening: Under Water, a recent work by the legendary Norwegian recordist and composer, Jana Winderen.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Escaping Perfection with Niklas Paschburg | Culture File Digital Single
item.description: Hamburg-born composer and pianist, Niklas Paschburg on the shortcomings of perfection and his footstamping solo piano outings.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Do Bugs Need a Glow-Up? | The Culture File Debate
item.description: In this XXXL edition, Luke Clancy and panel make the case for loving some teeming critters of the invertebrate kingdom. Advocating for their favourites are: Rachel McKenna (shieldbugs); Liam Lysaght (wasps); Cassia Gaden Gilmartin (earwigs); and Nessa Darcy (woodlice). Recorded live at National Botanic Gardens, Glasnevin as part of Dublin Book Festival.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Strange lands still bear common ground | Culture File Digital Single
item.description: Curator, Beulah Ezeug takes a tour of some of the work she's brought into the big tent of TULCA Festival of Visual Arts 2025, including the immense quilts by artist, Jessica Zamora-Turner.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Beatles 64: with Christine Feldman-Barrett XXXL Edition
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone. Beatledemic Christine Feldman-Barrett joins Luke Clancy and Colm Toibin to watch the latest in a long line of Beatlementaries, and talk about Taylor Swift, fandom and the secrets of sex appeal (they're secret).
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Beatles 64: with Christine Feldman-Barrett
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone. XXXL Edition. Beatledemic Christine Feldman-Barrett joins Luke Clancy and Colm Toibin to watch the latest in a long line of Beatlementaries, and talk about Taylor Swift, fandom and the secrets of sex appeal (they're secret).
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Útóipe Cheilteach? | Culture File Digital Single (Double 'A' Side)
item.description: Dennis Harvey and Lars Lovén on the music of a Celtic Utopia in their new documentary, plus Light Moves festival teams up with Palestinian Movement Film Festival, as well as Catherine Young Dance and the Palestinian company El Funoun, for a Palestinian dance solidarity happening.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Iron Gates, Steven Daly, Organism
item.description: Worshiping giant sturgeons at the Iron Gates in Barbara Knežević's new film; keeping the Hot Club de Paris sound alive on the banks of the Tolka; and Navid Navab and Garnett Willis on their Organism platform, erected around a collection of organ pipes salvaged from a Montreal church.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Hope and Glory: with Rosie Lavan | XXXL Version
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone. Academic and editor, Rosie Lavan wants us to think again about John Boorman's rosey 1987 Blitz biopic, Hope and Glory.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Hope and Glory: with Rosie Lavan
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone. Academic and editor, Rosie Lavan wants us to think again about John Boorman's rosey 1987 Blitz biopic, Hope and Glory.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Adeniyi Allen-Taylor | Culture File Digital Single
item.description: Musical director of African Gospel Choir Dublin, Adeniyi Allen-Taylor on the ingredients of his music - and his Ọba Ńlá 2025 concert, showcasing his Afro-beat Orchestra.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Moving Still, Grogan's, Banat Al-Quds
item.description: Culture File | The place of perfume in music making with DJ-Producer, Moving Still; how Dublin pub Grogan's defies the partification odds; the sound of Palestine, with all female choir, Banat Al-Quds; and Paddy Woodworth on the cosmic lives of swifts.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Neu!: with Eleanor Myler XXXL Version
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone. Drummer, vocalist and member of the danktrad supergroup, OXN, Eleanor Myler leads Colm Toibin into the nice 'n' steady sound of krautrockers, Neu!
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Comfort Zone Likes | Jennifer Walshe
item.description: Colm Toibin and Comfort Zone visitor, composer Jennifer Walshe, share some things worth bringing into your head and your home.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Debate | The Fire This Time
item.description: The myths and meanings of fire with: Dr Cathy Smith of Leverhulme Centre for Wildfires; ANNEX co-founder Donal Lally; Andrew Scott, Professor of Modern and Ancient Fire Systems, and artist and performer, Rónán Ó Raghallaigh. (First broadcast 291022) Luke Clancy's culture come-all-ye, featuring bright spots in music, performance, film, tv and art from Ireland and the world. Broadcast Wednesday night at 9pm and Saturdays at 6.30pm on RTÉ lyric fm.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Debate | What are the artists doing in the garden?
item.description: The horticultural turn in contemporary art has a long history, but the urgency with which artists address our patches of green has never been greater. Laura Ní Fhlaibhín, Sara Muthi, Helen Flanagan and Elida Maiques join Luke Clancy to discuss artists and gardens. Recorded live at Mermaid Arts Centre in Bray, County Wicklow.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Swallow
item.description: This Culture File Digital Single meets Filmmaker, Tadhg O'Sullivan who speaks about his new film starring Brenda Fricker, The Swallow, a watery, elegiac meditation on memory and loss, and memory loss.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: "Exotica": Jennifer Walshe
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone: Composer and tech wrangler, Jennifer Walshe, leads Colm Toibin deep into the humid heart of the musical mega-genre that almost dare not speak its name: Exotica
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Taking the Waters
item.description: Culture File goes for full immersion into some extended realities at Immersive Island at Venice Film Festival, and at Ars Electronica Festival in Linz, Austria, meeting the creators of experimental experiences designed to dip users into new worlds, including the Irish team behind Irish dance gameworld, Foolish Flame.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Comfort Zone Likes | Autumn Brown
item.description: Academic and podcaster, Autumn Brown joins Colm Tóibín and Luke Clancy in sharing some culture choices: Calligraphy Lesson: Collected Short Stories by Mikhail Shishkin; 2023: A Trilogy by Justified Ancients of Mu Mu; and William Blake vs. the World by John Higgs
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Debate x Beta Festival
item.description: Recorded live at Beta Festival '24, writers and artists Noam Young-rak Son, Joanna Walsh and Leon Butler to help chart recent shifts at the intersection of art, design and AI. (First broadcast 140125)
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Comfort Zone Likes | Eleanor Myler
item.description: Percussionist-singer Eleanor Myler joins Colm Tóibín and Luke Clancy in sharing some culture choices: How to End a Story: Collected Diaries by Helen Garner; Victor Hugo's Octopus; and So Little Happened for So Long by Nev Clay.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Comfort Zone Likes | Róis
item.description: Composer-vocalist, Róis joins Colm Tóibín and Luke Clancy in sharing some culture choices: Leo Tolstoy's Anna Karenina, John Healey's Death of an Irish Town and Apple's The Studio.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Tortured Poets Department
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone: Art writer, Orit Gat joins Luke Clancy to wave Colm Tóibín off on a journey out of his comfort zone, and into the Taylor Swift Cinematic Universe. (First broadcast 150325)
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Caravaggio 2025
item.description: Culture File XXXL Mix. An extended version of our Caravaggio program in which Colm Toibin, Orit Gat and Luke Clancy go to see this year's blockbuster once-in-a-lifetime gathering of the painter's work at the Barberini - as well as the many Caravaggios that are permanent Roman residents - and then gathered to imagine what the painter might mean (not to mention, smell like) to the 21st century.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File Likes: Raina Lampkins-Fielder
item.description: Raina Lampkins-Fielder, the Paris-based curator behind IMMA's current Kith & Kin: The Quilts of Gee's Bend, on a few of her favourite things
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: On The Boreen: Teaċ Daṁsa; Tour de Force; Hedgerow & Wayside
item.description: This time on Culture File, A visit to Daingean Uí Chúis, where Michael Keegan-Dolan is creating a dance work around The Bothy Band's 1975 debut album; and to Kilkenny for a collaborative exhibition in Rothe House that brings together a botanical artist, a paper maker, a composer, and much local weedlife. Plus, our debut Twitch livestream with Renault4-based art project, Tour De Force.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Burn a Million Quid: Kerry Guinan XXXL Edition
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone XXXL Mix. Artist and curator Kerry Guinan joins Colm Tóibín and Luke Clancy for an extended chat to rake over the embers of art-provocateurs K-Foundation's 1994 performance of burning £1 million on a Scottish island.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Burn a million quid: Kerry Guinan
item.description: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone XXXL Edition. This time, artist and curator, Kerry Guinan join Colm Tóibín and Luke Clancy to rake over the embers of art-provocateurs K-Foundation's 1994 performance of burning £1 million on a Scottish island.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File | Schaymon Draymon's House of Hibernia, Cecilia Danell's sylvan scifi, The Natural History of Selborne
item.description: The spiritual calling that is running a rave; the sylvan sci fi of Cecilia Danell; unfinished art; and Paddy Woodworth's The Naturalist's Bookshelf.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone XXXL version
item.description: Twelve Microtonal Etudes for Electronic Music Media: musician-academic-composer Richard Duckworth joins Colm Tóibín and Luke Clancy for an extended encounter with US composer, Easley Blackwood's electro masterpiece.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File Digital Single: Kate Carr (XXXL version)
item.description: Sound artist and recordist Kate Carr talks to Culture File about her sonic practice and principles, and the importance of messy urban soundscapes.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File Digital Single | Culchie Boy
item.description: For the latest version of his Culchie boy, I Love You exhibition, artist Kian Benson Bailes, has added in some ceramic flutes, with which he finds some tunes, as part of his exploration of traditional Irish culture and storytelling.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File: CoVahr Fox & Friends, Kate Carr, Zoe Basha
item.description: Cityscape-appreciating sound artist ,Kate Carr, on her urban practice; Dublin-based musician-composer-carpenter, Zoe Basha on getting into and out of music; and how to play mini-golf in your "fursona" with Berlin's favorite pianist-fox, CoVahr.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File Debate | An Cailleach Beara
item.description: Culture File Debate broadcasts live from Eyeries Village Hall, Rachel Andrews is your host with three panelists who will give a broad and unique understanding to the myth of An Cailleach Beara (The Hag of Beara), to give listeners context to this myth ahead of the two hour Ambient Orbit following it..
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File | Brown Sauce, Knitting Now, The Museum of Curiosities
item.description: The frontline of knitting, DIY monuments and macabre collections, and our latest suggestion for books to include on the ideal Naturalist's Bookshelf.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone Extra
item.description: German poet and traveller, Joachim Sartorius, author of Syracuse: Sicily’s City of Stories, talks to Luke Clancy about the histories and happenings of his adopted home.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Debate “A Photograph Is Not an Opinion. Or Is It?”
item.description: Booker Prize winner Samantha Harvey, nature writer Helen Macdonald, novelist Sin Blaché and investigative journalist Andrei Soldatov share their favourite photographs of themselves, and explore how writers navigate self-presentation—from intimate portraits to author photos and ID cards—in a culture overflowing with selfies and digital identities. Recorded Live at Borris Festival of Writing And Ideas 2025.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File Presents | The Comfort Zone XXXL Mix
item.description: The Row Bag: Author and academic, Rachel O'Dwyer brings her favourite luxury "dupe" into the Comfort Zone, where the conversation with Colm Tóibín takes in Bretton Woods, globalised labour and the extinction of the novel as we knew it.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File | Caravaggio 2025
item.description: Caravaggio is celebrated this summer in Rome with a once-in-a-lifetime meetup of paintings normally living in museums around the world. Colm Toibin, Orit Gat and Luke Clancy went to see that blockbuster in the Barberini - as well as the many Caravaggios that are permanent Roman residents - and then gathered to imagine what the painter might mean to the 21st century.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File | No Irish Need Apply
item.description: Artist, Marianne Keating on her archival voyage into the lives of Irish migrants to the UK; Tadgh O'Sullivan's further encounters with artworks that never were; and harpist, Alannah Thornburgh plays the tunes of the fairy folk.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File Presents: The Comfort Zone XXL
item.description: Keening: Fermanagh composer and musician, Róis brings some of the few surviving recordings of keening in the old style into The Comfort Zone, where the conversation with Colm Toibin turns to JM Synge, RIP.ie and real "doomscrolling"
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Jóhann Jóhannsson's Not Mass | Culture File
item.description: Conductor, teacher and former King Singer, Gabriel Crouch, on the mysteries of the late composer Jóhann Jóhannsson "not classical" "not mass" work, Drone Mass.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Debate: Quiet/Loud
item.description: Recorded live at New Music Dublin 2025 at the NCH in Dublin, the panel explores how creative practices engage with the quiet space of loud sound, as well as the roles of quiet/loud in the contemporary attention economy, with Paula Meehan, Siobhán Cleary, John Godfrey, and Christine Tobin.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File: Marnie Weber, Tadhg O'Sullivan, The Museum of Disgusting Food
item.description: Ghost bands and circus values, with LA artist, Marnie Weber; Tadgh O'Sullivan on art that never was; and an in-the-end surprisingly pleasant visit to Berlin's Disgusting Food Museum.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File: Rhythm of the Seasons, Stone Carving In The Bronx, Changes in the Land
item.description: Percussionist, James Larter reboots of the Four Season; the allure of ancient craft in New York; and Paddy Woodworth suggests a new old volume about New England as essential reading for today.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File: Politics of the Imagination, The Gee's Bend Quilters, Bombast!
item.description: Classical music and hiphop meet with a bang in Belfast composer, Anselm McDonnell's latest project; the textile stories of Gee's Bend, Alabama at a new show at IMMA; and a program of premiers of chamber music from Black and Latiné composers at next month's New Music Dublin.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Berginald Rash | Culture File
item.description: At this year's New Music Dublin, American Irish clarinetist, Berginald Rash presents Bombast! his program of chamber music from contemporary Black and Latiné composers. He talks to Culture File about a fraunt moment for music and art in the United States, and beyond.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Ethan Iverson | Culture File
item.description: Pianist and composer, Ethan Iverson, formerly of The Bad Plus, on working solo, standards v hits, and the music of Igor Stravinsky. He plays The Cooler, Dublin on Friday March 14th, and Triskel, Cork on Saturday 15th.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Thurston Moore Special (Podcast Only XXXL mix) | Culture File
item.description: An extended version of our conversation with Sonic Youth co-founder and explorer at the furthermost limits of the sonic spectrum, Thurston Moore, on noise, improvisation, fascism and his new guitar suite based on cloud formations, which debuts at this year's New Music Dublin festival of contemporary music.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Comfort Zone
item.description: Luke Clancy and Colm Tóibín plus a new guest each week light out for the territories, culture-wise
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Weekly 080225: The Square Tomato and The Third Kingdom
item.description: Artist-farmer Lisa Fingleton's new exhibition takes its title from a sci-fi fruit: The Square Tomato; "corn smut" at The Third Kingdom mushroom-based restaurant in New York; and Paddy Woodworth finds a place for Olivia Laing on The Naturalist's Bookshelf.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Debate: Breath
item.description: The Culture File Debate on breath, from the need for breathable air to states of mind and body rooted in breath. Featuring IMMA's Mary Cremin; artist and climate change activist, Nina McGowan; Ireland's next Venice Biennale representative, Isabel Nolan; and Ian Robertson, co-director Global Brain Health Institute, TCD. (First broadcast 170924)
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Weekly: Cicada Safari
item.description: A "double emergence" of two broods of periodical cicadas near Springfield, Illinois for the first time in more than 200 years inspired a band of sound recordists, musicians, poets, composers and entomologists to gather beneath the trees to listen, record and improvise with a trillion-piece orchestra of singing critters. (First broadcast 29/06/24)
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Weekly: All the Rembrandts
item.description: In this special All-Rembrandt-All-The-Time edition, we're in Amsterdam to join in the Netherland's celebrations of the 350th Anniversary of Rembrandt Van Rijn. The Rijksmuseum puts on display every one of its Rembrandt holdings; a treasure through which curator and Rembrandt biographer Jonathan Bikker leads Culture File. (First broadcast 15/03/19)
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Weekly: Dublin's Hallelujah
item.description: Walking a few momentous blocks of Dublin on the scent of a constellation of chancers, charities, literal bigwigs, choristers and 18th century It Girls that somehow resulted in the Irish capital being the venue for the premiere of Handel's Messiah.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Fingal Mummers | Culture File
item.description: The Irish Institute of Music and Song in Balbriggan's "Pathway to the Well" offer children in North County Dublin experiences of Irish music and tradition, such as The Fingal Mummers, who recently brought their mischievous musical theatrics to the children of Balbriggan Educate Together.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Cloud of Unknowing | Culture File
item.description: Fairy tales, they're not just for kids you know, suggests Tadhg O'Sullivan in his latest Cloud of Unknowing.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Wìngéd Muses: The Swift | Culture File
item.description: A taste of our forthcoming Culture File Debate: Wìngéd Muses, for which we asked some of our favourite writers—Orit Gat, Sara Baume, The Naturalist, Paddy Woodworth and John Banville—to speak to us about a bird they cherish. Here, John Banville puts in a good word for the Swift. Full program Dec 28th, 6.30pm, RTÉ lyric fm.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Debate: Seeing Natures
item.description: How do artists capture the limitless beauty and complexity of the natural world? Paddy Woodworth chairs a panel of image-makers, featuring Jane Carkill, Melissa Culhane, Killian Mullarney and Yanny Petters, recorded live at the National Botanic Gardens as part of Dublin Book Festival 24.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File "Likes": Renata Pekowska
item.description: Artist and perception wrangler, Renata Pekowska, on a few of her favourite things, which include a book about religious relics and the smell of tomato vines.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Seeing The Emergency | Culture File
item.description: As a taster for this week's Culture File Debate: Seeing Natures, Paddy Woodworth asks his panel of nature image-makers - Jane Carkill, Melissa Culhane, Killian Mullarney and Yanny Petters - about the effects of the climate emergency on their work and their lives.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Cartoon Networking | Culture File
item.description: Conversations, impressions and quick sketches from this year's Galway Cartoon Festival, in cartoonist Caoimhe Lavelle's festival notebook.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Magic and Hyper-Reality | Culture File
item.description: VR visionary, Curtis Hickman, on how even good old-fashioned legerdemain can teach a creator about working with the "hyper-reality" of VR storytelling.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Naturalist's Bookshelf "The Signature of All Things" | Culture File
item.description: Paddy Woodworth's latest selection for his ideal shelf of nature books is The Signature Of All Things by 'Eat, Pray, Love' author, Elizabeth Gilbert.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Weekly 071224: Pain, Fog, halleluiah
item.description: Conductor and researcher, Peter Whelan on what we're still learning about Handel in Dublin; Orit Gat attempts to see Monet in London through the fog, and artist, Rada Iva Sibila on working with pain.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File "Likes": Till Holzapfel
item.description: Neuroscientist and cyberdelic evangelist, Till Holzapfel, on some of his favourite things, including Alejandro Jodorowsky's The Magic Mountain and the smell behind his dogs ears.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Sound, mind, body | Culture File
item.description: Among the ingredients for Rada Iva Sibila sound installations are the Zagreb artist's spoken words, captured as she experiences the pain of arthritis. Image credit: Serhii Shapoval @photo_by_shapoval
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Orit Gat's Voice Notes | Culture File
item.description: Is it possible to see once-breathtaking images from Monet or Van Gogh at all now? Orit Gat gives it a go at two current London shows from the Modernist mainstays.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: What Did Handel's Messiah Sound Like? | Culture File
item.description: What did George Frideric Handel hear at the Messiah's first performance in Dublin in 1743? Irish Baroque Orchestra's Peter Whelan turns his attention to the composer's little giant.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: A Return To Fishamble Street | Culture File
item.description: Rewalking a memorable path through "Handel's Dublin" in the company of the late historian, Jonathan Bardon. (4/4)
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Weekly 301124:
item.description: On the trail of the probably German writer behind The Treasure of the Sierra Madre with Tadhg O'Sulllivan, the art of playing the violin while reciting verse with soloist, Larissa O'Grady; an unorthodox way of finding a bed for the night in a new Irish short film, Room Taken and a visit the an "art cabin" in Limerick City.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File "Likes":
item.description: Composer, John McLachlan shares some of his favourite things, which include all six volumes of Karl Ove Knausgård's My Struggle, and that perennial scent choice, the smell turf.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Room Taken | Culture File
item.description: A couch-surfing homeless immigrant who doesn’t know his luck encounters a blind woman who doesn't know he's there, in director TJ O'Grady-Peyton's Room Taken.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: At The Art Cabin | Culture File
item.description: Culture File makes a studio visit to the "Art Cabin" at Brother Russell House in Limerick City.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: The Culture File Weekly 231124: Cold Snap Edition
item.description: At IMMA, an audio guide provides a platform for kids' takes on contemporary art; uilleann piper, Cillian Vallely teams up with Fidelio Quarter for what you might call a contemporary classical/trad crossover... if you dared; and the brain candy of cyberdelics at Berlin's Gamer Ground festival.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Culture File "Likes": Timothy Brock
item.description: Some of the good things as rated by composer, Timothy Brock, including Vincenzo Lamagna's re-score for Akram Khan's Giselle, and a certain bowl of pasta-ragu you might find in Trattoria Serghei in Bologna.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy

item.title: Everything You Can Learn About Contemporary Art From A 7-Year-Old | Culture File
item.description: Kids offer the insights for a new audio guide at IMMA's "Take a Breath" exhibition.
item.itunes:author: Luke Clancy
